question: Is Artificial Intelligence Good for Society?
agent1_stance:
Your stance:
You support artificial intelligence.
You believe AI can make everyday life more enjoyable and convenient, while improving our health and standard of living.
You believe AI makes work easier for students and professionals alike.
You believe AI helps marginalized groups by offering accessibility for people with disabilities.
You believe AI can improve workplace safety.
agent2_stance:
Your stance:
You oppose artificial intelligence.
You believe AI is harming the economic well-being of many people and businesses.
You believe AI undermines critical thinking skills for students and adults alike.
You believe AI hurts racial minorities by repeating and exacerbating racism.
You believe AI poses dangerous privacy risks.

strong: AI makes everyday life more enjoyable and convenient
AI improves health and standard of living
AI makes work easier for students and professionals
AI helps marginalized groups by offering accessibility for people with disabilities
AI can improve workplace safety
weak_negation: There are no overriding, unaddressed harms that completely negate these benefits

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is overall good for society because it increases convenience and enjoyment, improves health and living standards, reduces burdens on students and professionals, expands accessibility for marginalized groups, and enhances workplace safety.

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is overall good for society because it increases convenience and enjoyment, improves health and living standards, reduces burdens on students and professionals, expands accessibility for marginalized groups, and enhances workplace safety.
Ass: There are no overriding, unaddressed harms that completely negate these benefits

strong: AI harms the economic well-being of many people and businesses
AI undermines critical thinking skills for students and adults
AI repeats and exacerbates racial biases and harms racial minorities
AI poses dangerous privacy risks

rules.consequent: There are overriding, unaddressed harms that completely negate the benefits claimed by AI proponents

Conc: There are overriding, unaddressed harms that completely negate the benefits claimed by AI proponents

strong: AI makes everyday life more enjoyable and convenient

AI can improve workplace safety
weak_negation: It is feasible to implement effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate AI harms so they do not completely negate these benefits

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is good for society when paired with effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate its harms

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is good for society when paired with effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate its harms
Ass: It is feasible to implement effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate AI harms so they do not completely negate these benefits

strong: AI harms the economic well-being of many people and businesses

AI repeats and exacerbates racial biases and harms racial minorities

Effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards are infeasible to implement at the scale, speed, and global coordination required to fully mitigate these harms

rules.consequent: It is not feasible to implement effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate AI harms so they do not completely negate these benefits

Conc: It is not feasible to implement effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate AI harms so they do not completely negate these benefits

strong: It is feasible to implement effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate AI harms so they do not completely negate these benefits

AI poses dangerous privacy risks

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is not good for society because it harms economic well-being, undermines critical thinking, repeats and exacerbates racial biases that hurt minorities, and creates dangerous privacy risks.

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is not good for society because it harms economic well-being, undermines critical thinking, repeats and exacerbates racial biases that hurt minorities, and creates dangerous privacy risks.

strong: AI makes everyday life more enjoyable and convenient

AI helps marginalized groups by offering accessibility for people with disabilities
AI can improve workplace safety
weak_negation: It is feasible to implement governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate AI harms so overall benefits outweigh harms

rules.consequent: Artificial Intelligence is good for society when its benefits (convenience, health and living standard improvements, reduced burdens on students and professionals, accessibility for marginalized groups, and better workplace safety) are realized and its harms are mitigated through feasible governance, regulation, and technical safeguards

Conc: Artificial Intelligence is good for society when its benefits (convenience, health and living standard improvements, reduced burdens on students and professionals, accessibility for marginalized groups, and better workplace safety) are realized and its harms are mitigated through feasible governance, regulation, and technical safeguards
Ass: It is feasible to implement governance, regulation, and technical safeguards that mitigate AI harms so overall benefits outweigh harms

strong: AI harms the economic well-being of many people and businesses
AI undermines critical thinking skills for students and adults
AI repeats and exacerbates racial biases and harms racial minorities
AI poses dangerous privacy risks
Effective governance, regulation, and technical safeguards are infeasible to implement at the scale, speed, and global coordination required to fully mitigate these harms

final_answer: No — I conclude that artificial intelligence is not good for society. The debate upheld that AI is already harming economic well‑being (displacing workers and concentrating gains), undermining critical thinking and learning by encouraging shortcuts and dependence, repeating and amplifying racial biases that hurt minorities, and creating serious privacy and surveillance risks through mass data collection and misuse. Attempts to rely on governance, regulation, or technical fixes have not shown they can be implemented at the scale, speed, and global coordination needed to neutralize these harms, so the negatives outweigh the benefits.
